description.task: Implement a DoublyLinkedList class with Node having both next and prev pointers. Support insertFront, insertBack, deleteFront, deleteBack, and bidirectional traversal (printForward and printBackward).
instructions: insertBack(val): if tail==null set head=tail=new Node(val); else create Node, set tail.next=node, node.prev=tail, tail=node; size++
deleteFront(): save head.val; if head==tail set head=tail=null; else head=head.next; head.prev=null; size--; return val
deleteBack(): save tail.val; if head==tail set head=tail=null; else tail=tail.prev; tail.next=null; size--; return val
printForward/printBackward are provided — do not modify
After insertBack(1,2,3) and insertFront(0): FWD 0<->1<->2<->3, BWD 3<->2<->1<->0

title: LRU Cache with Doubly Linked List

description.task: Implement a simplified LRUCache using a doubly linked list and a HashMap. The cache has a fixed capacity. On get(), move the accessed node to the front (most recently used). On put(), evict the tail (LRU) when over capacity.
instructions: get(key): if key not in map return -1; get node, call remove(n) then addFront(n); return n.val
put(key, value): if key in map, update node.val, remove then addFront; else if map.size()==capacity, remove tail.prev from map and DLL; create new Node(key,value), addFront, put in map
remove() and addFront() are provided — use them
Cache capacity=2: put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1)=1 makes key1 MRU; put(3,3) evicts key2 (LRU)
description.hint: get: remove(n); addFront(n); return n.val. put new: if(map.size()==capacity){Node lru=tail.prev; remove(lru); map.remove(lru.key);} Node n=new Node(key,value); addFront(n); map.put(key,n);

title: Browser History with Doubly Linked List

description.task: Implement a BrowserHistory class using a doubly linked list to support visit(url), back(), and forward() operations. Forward history is cleared when a new page is visited.
instructions: visit(url): Page p = new Page(url); p.prev = current; current.next = p; current = p; (p.next is already null)
back(): if current.prev != null, current = current.prev; return current.url
forward(): if current.next != null, current = current.next; return current.url
When visiting a new page, the forward chain from current is lost (current.next gets overwritten)
After visit(c.com) from a.com, forward() stays at c.com (no next)
description.hint: visit: Page p=new Page(url); p.prev=current; current.next=p; current=p; back: if(current.prev!=null) current=current.prev; return current.url;
description.expectedOutput:
Current: b.com
Back: a.com
Back: home.com
Forward: a.com
Forward after visit: c.com
